누가복음 5:1.content: Now while the multitude pressed on him and heard the word of God, he was standing by the lake of Gennesaret.

누가복음 5:2.content: He saw two boats standing by the lake, but the fishermen had gone out of them, and were washing their nets.

누가복음 5:3.content: He entered into one of the boats, which was Simon’s, and asked him to put out a little from the land. He sat down and taught the multitudes from the boat.

누가복음 5:4.content: When he had finished speaking, he said to Simon, “Put out into the deep, and let down your nets for a catch.”

누가복음 5:5.content: Simon answered him, “Master, we worked all night, and took nothing; but at your word I will let down the net.”

누가복음 5:6.content: When they had done this, they caught a great multitude of fish, and their net was breaking.

누가복음 5:7.content: They beckoned to their partners in the other boat, that they should come and help them. They came, and filled both boats, so that they began to sink.

누가복음 5:8.content: But Simon Peter, when he saw it, fell down at Jesus’ knees, saying, “Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, Lord.”

누가복음 5:9.content: For he was amazed, and all who were with him, at the catch of fish which they had caught;

누가복음 5:10.content: and so also were James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. Jesus said to Simon, “Don’t be afraid. From now on you will be catching people alive.”

누가복음 5:11.content: When they had brought their boats to land, they left everything, and followed him.

누가복음 5:12.content: While he was in one of the cities, behold, there was a man full of leprosy. When he saw Jesus, he fell on his face, and begged him, saying, “Lord, if you want to, you can make me clean.”

누가복음 5:13.content: He stretched out his hand, and touched him, saying, “I want to. Be made clean.” Immediately the leprosy left him.

누가복음 5:14.content: He commanded him to tell no one, “But go your way, and show yourself to the priest, and offer for your cleansing according to what Moses commanded, for a testimony to them.”

누가복음 5:15.content: But the report concerning him spread much more, and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.

누가복음 5:16.content: But he withdrew himself into the desert, and prayed.

누가복음 5:17.content: On one of those days, he was teaching; and there were Pharisees and teachers of the law sitting by, who had come out of every village of Galilee, Judea, and Jerusalem. The power of the Lord was with him to heal them.

누가복음 5:18.content: Behold, men brought a paralyzed man on a cot, and they sought to bring him in to lay before Jesus.

누가복음 5:19.content: Not finding a way to bring him in because of the multitude, they went up to the housetop, and let him down through the tiles with his cot into the middle before Jesus.

누가복음 5:20.content: Seeing their faith, he said to him, “Man, your sins are forgiven you.”

누가복음 5:21.content: The scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, “Who is this that speaks blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?”

누가복음 5:22.content: But Jesus, perceiving their thoughts, answered them, “Why are you reasoning so in your hearts?

누가복음 5:23.content: Which is easier to say, ‘Your sins are forgiven you;’ or to say, ‘Arise and walk?’

누가복음 5:24.content: But that you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins” (he said to the paralyzed man), “I tell you, arise, take up your cot, and go to your house.”

누가복음 5:25.content: Immediately he rose up before them, and took up that which he was laying on, and departed to his house, glorifying God.

누가복음 5:26.content: Amazement took hold on all, and they glorified God. They were filled with fear, saying, “We have seen strange things today.”

누가복음 5:27.content: After these things he went out, and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the tax office, and said to him, “Follow me!”

누가복음 5:28.content: He left everything, and rose up and followed him.

누가복음 5:29.content: Levi made a great feast for him in his house. There was a great crowd of tax collectors and others who were reclining with them.

누가복음 5:30.content: Their scribes and the Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, “Why do you eat and drink with the tax collectors and sinners?”

누가복음 5:31.content: Jesus answered them, “Those who are healthy have no need for a physician, but those who are sick do.

누가복음 5:32.content: I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”

누가복음 5:33.content: They said to him, “Why do John’s disciples often fast and pray, likewise also the disciples of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink?”

누가복음 5:34.content: He said to them, “Can you make the friends of the bridegroom fast while the bridegroom is with them?

누가복음 5:35.content: But the days will come when the bridegroom will be taken away from them. Then they will fast in those days.”

누가복음 5:36.content: He also told a parable to them. “No one puts a piece from a new garment on an old garment, or else he will tear the new, and also the piece from the new will not match the old.

누가복음 5:37.content: No one puts new wine into old wine skins, or else the new wine will burst the skins, and it will be spilled, and the skins will be destroyed.

누가복음 5:38.content: But new wine must be put into fresh wine skins, and both are preserved.

누가복음 5:39.content: No man having drunk old wine immediately desires new, for he says, ‘The old is better.’ ”
